title: AMQP 0-9-1 Model, Virtual Hosts, Exchanges, Queues, and Bindings · Compiler Case: Zero Many Route Evidence
summary: A diagram sends publishers directly to shared queues, confuses exchanges with storage, ignores virtual-host isolation, and cannot explain how routing keys and bindings produce zero, one, or many destinations. Planned artifact: Add and verify the zero many route evidence increment in AMQP entity and routing graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prove unroutable, single-route, fan-out, and overlapping multi-route outcomes with an explicit topology table.
Derive destination sets from exchange type, routing key, binding key, headers, and exchange-to-exchange bindings.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Zero Many Route Evidence · Theory Zero Many Route Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 8395 in a recovery drill with stale callbacks from an old channel generation. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive zero, one, or many queue destinations from exchange and binding evidence; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
